_comment: Moving wheel (cavalry, closes #922) vs the existing halt-about-face-standing-wheel-march composite (infantry), side by side on the same oblique rear-sector geometry (~140 degrees off facing at the moment the turn is ordered -- matching test_wheel_turn_maneuver.gd's own standard case) so the difference is directly visible in one clip. Both units first march straight ahead to build real cruising speed, then are ordered onto the same sharply-angled destination mid-march. The cavalry (left) gallops through a single continuous wheel while still advancing -- no halt, no about-face; the infantry (right) visibly halts, about-faces in place, standing-wheels the remaining residual, then marches. (The cavalry's own subsequent march keeps gradually re-aiming toward the literal ordered point as its long, ground-covering wheel carries it well off the original line -- verified via dump-state.sh, not asserted as a fixed final bearing -- while the infantry's much smaller wheel residual leaves it settled on close to the original 140 degrees throughout; see sparta-demos.md's 'verify a demo thoroughly' entry.) Fresh scenario authored for this demo -- see .claude/memories/sparta-demos.md's 'author each demo scenario fresh' entry -- not copied from any existing wheel/pivot input script.
